name: Party NPC Victory: Guard Kills Last Enemy While Fighter is Dying
description: Tests the victory condition when a party-faction NPC deals the killing blow. A Fighter (1 HP) and a Guard NPC (faction: party) fight a Goblin. Fighter passes their turn immediately. Guard and Goblin both act. Guard is adjacent to Goblin with a strong attack (min 7 damage) versus Goblin's 7 HP — guarantees a one-shot kill. Even if Goblin attacks Fighter first (reducing them to 0 HP / dying), the Guard killing the last monster must trigger Victory (not Defeat), because the combat-victory-policy treats faction='party' NPCs as allies and a dying Fighter still counts as alive (isDying: hp<=0, deathSaves.failures < 3). Validates: (a) party NPC kills can end combat, (b) Defeat does not fire when PC is dying but party NPC is alive.

npcs.comment: Gareth the Guard: faction=party so he counts as an ally for victory/defeat policy. Adjacent to Goblin at (10,15). Attack +8 does 1d8+6 (min 7) which one-shots the 7 HP Goblin on any non-nat-1 roll.
npcs.name: Gareth the Guard

monsters.comment: Goblin Skirmisher: 7 HP so any solid hit from Gareth kills it. AC 8 so the guard's d20+8 vs AC 8 hits on d20 >= 0 (only nat 1 misses). Adjacent to both Fighter at (15,10) and Guard at (10,15).
monsters.name: Goblin Skirmisher

actions.comment: Fighter rolls 20 initiative → 20+0 (DEX mod) = 20. Fighter goes first.
Fighter is at 1 HP and can't afford to take a hit. Pass the turn immediately to let the Guard and Goblin act.

Wait for all AI turns: Goblin Skirmisher and Gareth the Guard both act. Two possible outcomes: (A) Guard attacks first → kills Goblin → Victory. (B) Goblin attacks Fighter first (min 4 damage kills 1 HP Fighter) → Fighter dying → Guard attacks Goblin → kills it → Victory. In both paths, combat ends with Victory because Guard (faction=party) is alive → not all allies dead.

Goblin Skirmisher must be dead. Victory fired because Guard (faction=party) dealt the killing blow or survived when Goblin was killed. Validates that party-faction NPCs count as allies for the victory/defeat evaluation.
